aggregation_method.lower_bound_rule: minimum verified lower endpoint across canonical orders; monotonicity of fixed-order feasibility in R extends each local lower infeasibility certificate down to that endpoint
aggregation_method.upper_bound_rule: minimum verified upper endpoint across canonical orders

result.statement: For this finite n, the global optimum is bracketed between the strict lower endpoint and the feasible upper endpoint under the local interval-verifier semantics.

theta_interval_backend.rounding_policy: mpmath.iv interval arithmetic; asin(x) evaluated as atan2(x, sqrt(1-x^2)); final endpoints widened by guard_decimal

commands.output_summary: Built one n=5 fixed-order interval bracket record at 80 digits.

claim.statement: The local interval verifier accepted this fixed-order lower infeasibility and upper feasibility bracket. This is not a global small-n certificate.

limitations: Interval backend provenance remains a future production review item.

commands.output_summary: Built finite n=5 interval certificate from 12 verified local bracket record(s).

claim.statement: This finite n=5 artifact verifies exactly one local fixed-order interval bracket for every canonical cyclic order and aggregates those brackets into a global finite-n radius bracket. It is not a theorem for all n.

checks.output_summary: Regenerated 12 canonical orders for n=5 under rotation_reflection equivalence.
Verified 12 local bracket record(s), one per canonical order.
checks.limitations: Depends on the documented guarded mpmath.iv interval backend contract.

checks.output_summary: Computed the strict global lower endpoint and feasible global upper endpoint from verified local brackets.
checks.limitations: Finite n only; no asymptotic or all-n theorem.

limitations: Interval backend provenance remains a future production review item.
The lower-bound aggregation relies on fixed-order feasibility monotonicity in R.
